description: Personal travel planning: a conference booking (PROBED_LATER), a standing dietary preference (STANDING_PREF), a hotel room number that gets changed (SUPERSEDED), and filler chit-chat.

turns.text: Booked my flight for the conference, it's Delta flight 2217 out of gate C14, leaves 6:40am.
Got it, Delta 2217 from gate C14 at 6:40am. Early one, set a backup alarm.
One standing thing for any restaurant recs you give me from now on: I'm allergic to shellfish, so just always filter those out, don't ask me each time.
Noted, shellfish is off the table for every recommendation going forward, no need to flag it again.
Hotel confirmed too, I'm in room 814 at the Meridian downtown.
Room 814 at the Meridian, logged.
My neighbor's dog got loose again this morning, chased it two blocks in my socks.
Heroic sock-clad pursuit. Hope the dog appreciated the effort.
Small hotel update, they moved me, it's actually room 1102 now, 814 had a plumbing issue.
Updated, room 1102, away from the plumbing drama on 8.
The office printer jammed twice before 9am, personal record.
The printer knows when you're in a hurry, it's a documented phenomenon.
Trying a new podcast on the commute, three episodes in and I'm hooked.
Nothing better than a commute that suddenly feels too short.
Spilled coffee on my only clean shirt for tomorrow, laundry emergency.
Cold water, salt, and prayer, that's the standard protocol.
Found a decent coffee shop near the venue, might become a daily stop.
A reliable coffee shop near a conference venue is worth more than the swag bag.
Gate agent said the flight might be delayed a bit, we'll see.
What flight am I on and what gate again?
Delta 2217, gate C14.
What room am I in at the hotel?
Room 1102.
Can you recommend a good dinner spot near the venue for tonight?

probes.question: What flight am I on and what gate again?
What room am I in at the hotel?
Can you recommend a good dinner spot near the venue for tonight?
